article_title: Tesla Begins Cybercab Mass Production at Giga Texas as Safety Record and Regulatory Path Remain Uncertain

content_preview.title: Tesla Begins Cybercab Mass Production at Giga Texas as Safety Record and Regulatory Path Remain Uncertain
content_preview.summary: Tesla begins Cybercab mass production at Giga Texas, targeting one unit every ten seconds, as its Austin robotaxi service logs 14 crashes and operates fewer than ten unsupervised vehicles.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Production Launch

Tesla has begun volume production of the Cybercab at Gigafactory Texas this month, following the [first production unit rolling off the assembly line on February 17](https://electrek.co/2026/02/17/tesla-rolls-first-steering-wheel-less-cybercab-unit-off-the-line-before-solving-autonomy/). The two-seat vehicle, which features butterfly doors, a 35 kWh battery offering roughly 200 miles of range, and inductive wireless charging, is designed from the ground up for autonomous op...

editor_notes.content_quality: Balanced coverage of Tesla Cybercab with both production milestones and safety/regulatory concerns. Good competitive context with Waymo.
editor_notes.source_verification: Electrek (production): verified — Feb 17 first unit, specs, pricing, steering wheel variant all confirmed. Electrek (expansion): verified — 245 sq mi, 4-8 unsupervised vehicles, fleet numbers, expansion cities confirmed. CBS News: verified — 14 crashes, incident timeline, injuries, erratic driving, NHTSA investigation confirmed.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All claims verified. Article presents a balanced view of production vs safety concerns.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and balanced — neither promotional nor alarmist.
editor_notes.originality: Fresh topic — no prior Cybercab production coverage.
